description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the $CI_REGISTRY/openlp/runners/angular Docker image and defines three stages: test, build, and deploy. In the test stage add an audit job (allow_failure: true, always runs) that executes `yarn install` then `yarn audit`; a lint job (always runs) that executes `yarn install` then `yarn lint`; and a test job (always runs) that executes `yarn install` then `yarn test --no-progress --no-watch --browsers=ChromiumHeadlessCI`. In the build stage add a build-branch job that runs only when the commit branch is not "master", runs `yarn install`, `yarn build --no-progress --configuration production --aot`, sets `APP_VERSION=$(git describe --dirty --tags --long --match '*[0-9]*')`, writes `window.appVersion = "$APP_VERSION";` to `dist/web-remote/assets/version.js`, and stores the `dist/` directory as an artifact; a build-tag job that runs only on tags, runs `yarn install`, `yarn build --no-progress --configuration production --aot`, writes `window.appVersion = "$CI_COMMIT_TAG";` to `dist/web-remote/assets/version.js`, and stores the `dist/` directory as an artifact; and a push-i18n-source job that runs when `src/assets/en.json` changes, installs npm via `apk add npm`, installs the Transifex CLI with `npm install @transifex/api`, and runs `npm run tx push`. In the deploy stage add a deploy job that runs only on tags, sets `OPENLP_HOST` to `new.openlp.io`, changes to `dist/web-remote`, creates a zip `remote-$CI_COMMIT_TAG.zip`, computes its SHA‑256 checksum and file size, generates a JSON manifest `version-$CI_COMMIT_TAG.json`, configures SSH using the `SSH_PRIVATE_KEY` secret, connects to `openlp@$OPENLP_HOST` to create the remote directory, uploads the zip and JSON files via `scp`, replaces the server’s `version.json` with the new manifest, and uses the computed checksum and size in the JSON.
